steps: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and sign in with your Google account
Select an existing project from the top dropdown or click 'New Project' to create one
Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/translate.googleapis.com and click 'Enable' to enable the Cloud Translation API
Click '+ Create Credentials' and select 'OAuth client ID'
If prompted, click 'Configure consent screen', choose 'External', fill in the app name and your email, then save
Back on the credentials page, click '+ Create Credentials' > 'OAuth client ID'
Select 'Web application' as the application type
Enter a name (e.g. 'Translation API Client'), add your redirect URI under 'Authorized redirect URIs', then click 'Create'
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the dialog that appears

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID from Google Cloud Console

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret from Google Cloud Console

notes: The Cloud Translation API requires billing to be enabled on your Google Cloud project
New Google Cloud accounts receive $300 in free credits; the Translation API also has a free tier of 500,000 characters per month
The OAuth consent screen must be configured before creating OAuth credentials
Add test users on the consent screen page if your app is in 'Testing' mode

test_endpoint.description: List all supported translation languages — no parameters required
